title: Auditing an existing codebase to back-fill decisions
question: How should the tool reconstruct a decision log for an app that was built without one?
rationale: Add a '/decision-tree audit <path>' mode that discovers, then confirms: it mines git history and scans static structure to draft load-bearing decisions, then interviews you to fill the 'why' before anything is written. It runs the normal confirm-at-the-moment flow in reverse, so the scan proposes and you dispose.

options.label: Hybrid skill mode: discover, then confirm

tradeoffs.note: reuses the existing viewer and confirm-at-the-moment flow
the interview step fills rationale the code alone can't show
scoped to load-bearing choices, not every detectable pick
three stages to build — history mine, static scan, interview
just walk commits, PRs, and reverts
only captures decisions that left a commit trail; considered-but-never-built choices are invisible
infer choices from manifest, config, and layout
recovers the what, not the why or the rejected options
risks 100+ low-value entries that bury the load-bearing ones
faster to build now; emits JSON the viewer already reads
not reusable across repos, not integrated into the skill or its flow
